course_title: Perspectives on Non-Western Art Topics: The Art of Early China
subject: ARTHIST
course_description: Focuses on one of several diverse, non-European art historical traditions, such as ancient Egypt, pre-Hispanic Americas, medieval Islam, Oceania, and sub-Saharan Africa. May be repeated for credit when topic changes.
course_notes: "The Art of Early China"What can ceramic, jade, bone, bronze, stone, or silk tell us about life and history in early China? How might modern archaeology complicate the traditional descriptions of Chinese culture? In what ways do contemporary biases inform our interpretations of the ancient material remains? This course explores significant works of art from the Neolithic period, the Bronze Age, and the early imperial periods (ca. 200 CE). We will closely analyze objects unearthed through archaeological excavations and compare them with related historical texts. Readings drawn from both academic scholarship and popular literature will complement our weekly lectures and discussions. Art History major / minor: Div. I or IV
